name: Alex Rivera
current_workflow: Writes rough bullet points in Notion, then spends 3 hours manually expanding them into a script in Google Docs, often missing deadlines and burning out before the Sunday drop.
personality: Tech-obsessed, fast-paced, willing to break things to make them work, treats the podcast as a primary business revenue stream.
triggers: Seeing a demo where an outline is instantly converted to a 'spoken-word' style draft in under 5 minutes.
objections: Nothing, except maybe 'Does it understand my specific niche jargon?'
current_tools: Notion, Riverside.fm, Descript, Spotify for Podcasters

name: Sarah Jenkins
current_workflow: Collaborates with a junior editor to flesh out outlines. They spend late nights refining the script to ensure it sounds natural, causing her to miss family time and increasing stress.
personality: Pragmatic, values time over features, skeptical of 'magic' solutions but desperate for efficiency to hire help sooner.
triggers: A clear ROI showing how the tool saves 10+ hours a week, allowing her to hire a part-time producer.
objections: Will the AI write in a generic, robotic tone? I need my unique voice intact.

name: Marcus Thorne
current_workflow: Uses a standard template in Word. He manually types the intro and outro, and asks the team to fill in the middle based on the outline. It's slow but reliable.
personality: Cautious, traditional, values stability and brand reputation over cutting-edge tech.
triggers: Social proof: Seeing a peer in their industry (a well-known podcast) endorse the tool and report consistent quality.
objections: I don't want to rely on AI for my intellectual property. If it hallucinates facts, I look bad.

name: David Chen
current_workflow: Records voice memos for ideas, transcribes them to get text, then spends hours editing and rewriting that text into a structured script. Feels like double work.
personality: Analytical, data-driven, loves tools that integrate seamlessly into his existing stack.
triggers: Integration with his existing Notion workspace and the ability to edit the script directly within the tool.
objections: Is this just another wrapper around a generic LLM? I need specific training data for my industry.

name: Elena Rodriguez
current_workflow: Overwhelmed by the release schedule. She manually expands her guest's raw notes into a script, often re-recording episodes because the flow feels clunky.
personality: Ambitious, early adopter of new workflows, willing to pay for speed to scale her show.
triggers: The ability to generate 'host-ready' drafts that she can tweak in minutes, not hours.
objections: None really, just wants the best speed-to-market possible.

name: Robert 'Rob' Miller
current_workflow: Sticks to a rigid 50-year-old outline format. He writes the script by hand on paper first, then types it out. He views AI as a threat to authenticity.
personality: Traditional, resistant to change, values craft and manual effort over automation.
triggers: A guarantee that the tool will not alter his tone or 'ruin' his legacy content.
objections: I won't use AI to write my thoughts. It feels cheap and impersonal.

name: Priya Patel
current_workflow: Struggles with consistency. One week she writes a 4-page script, the next she just reads a 1-page outline. She wants a middle ground that's easy to maintain.
personality: Balanced, seeks efficiency without sacrificing quality, practical and realistic.
triggers: Seeing a trial version that clearly saves time on the 'filler' content between outline points.
objections: Will it make me sound like a robot if I get lazy on the edits?

name: James Wilson
current_workflow: Tries to keep up with trends but drowns in content creation. Often skips writing the script entirely, leading to poor audio quality and low retention.
personality: Hype-driven, loves shiny objects, willing to experiment with anything that promises viral growth.
triggers: Buzzwords like 'viral hooks', 'AI voice optimization', and 'trending topics integration'.
objections: Only concerned if it's not the latest 'beta' version.
current_tools: TikTok, Discord, Spotify for Podcasters, various Discord bots

name: Linda Gomez
current_workflow: Has a dedicated writing assistant (intern) who types the script from her outline. She wants to reduce the intern's workload while maintaining high editorial standards.
personality: Corporate, risk-averse, focused on team productivity metrics.
triggers: Case studies showing reduced burnout rates among her writing staff.
objections: Is the data secure? Can we sign an NDA with the vendor?
current_tools: Salesforce, Microsoft Teams, Adobe Premiere Pro, Shownotes Pro

name: Tom Baker
current_workflow: Records interviews, transcribes them, then manually rewrites the transcript into a script because he hates reading raw transcripts.
personality: Smart, busy, appreciates tools that do the 'boring' work so he can focus on the ideas.
triggers: A tool that can intelligently summarize interview transcripts into an engaging script format.
objections: I need to retain full copyright ownership; no weird licensing clauses.

name: Grace Lee
current_workflow: Panics about the first draft. Writes a vague outline, then spends hours worrying if it's 'good enough' before hitting record.
personality: Perfectionist, anxious, easily discouraged by blank screens.
triggers: A 'start writing' button that removes the blank page fear and provides a strong opening hook.
objections: Will it sound too formal? I need to sound like a friendly peer.

name: Kevin O'Brien
current_workflow: Works with a small team. They use a shared Google Doc where he pastes the outline and they fill it in. It's clunky and version-controlled poorly.
triggers: Ease of use: 'Does it work in a browser without installing anything complex?'
objections: Our team isn't tech-savvy; the tool needs to be incredibly intuitive.

name: Sophie Martin
current_workflow: Uses a mix of rough sketches and typed notes. She finds the transition from visual ideas to text script difficult and time-consuming.
personality: Creative, visual thinker, frustrated by linear text tools.
triggers: A tool that understands visual descriptions and converts them into vivid spoken-word scripts.
objections: Nothing, as long as it respects the creative flow.

name: Frank Russo
current_workflow: Uses a printed template he made in the 90s. He fills it in with a pen. He rarely uses digital tools for scripting.
personality: Old school, resistant, believes in the 'sweat of the brow'.
triggers: Almost none. Maybe a very cheap free tier that doesn't require login.
objections: I don't need AI. I need a better pen or a faster printer.

name: Nina Kowalski
current_workflow: Tries to write scripts while listening to industry news, but context-switching kills her productivity. She wants to automate the drafting.
personality: High-energy, fast-paced, always looking for an edge.
triggers: Real-time summarization of news feeds integrated directly into the script outline.
objections: Will it know the latest crypto slang and memes automatically?

name: Bill Henderson
current_workflow: Wants to scale the show but feels guilty about using AI. He tries to do it manually but admits it's unsustainable.
personality: Guilt-ridden about automation, values human connection, slow to trust.
triggers: Marketing that emphasizes 'AI as a co-pilot' rather than 'AI replacing the writer'.
objections: Is this ethical? Am I cheating my audience?

name: Rachel Green
current_workflow: Manages a team of volunteers. They submit rough ideas, and she spends her weekends editing them into scripts. She's overworked.
personality: Empathetic, overworked, needs to protect her team's mental health.
triggers: Proof that the tool reduces the editing time by 60%, allowing her team to take breaks.
objections: Will it lose the empathetic, gentle tone required for our niche?

name: Steve 'The Shark' Davis
current_workflow: Misses deadlines constantly. Uses a free account for everything. Willing to pay anything to get back on track.
triggers: Speed. Immediate conversion from outline to script. 'Get me a draft NOW'.
objections: None. Just needs it to work immediately.
current_tools: Canva, Google Drive, Riverside, generic free AI tools

name: Amanda Foster
current_workflow: Writes short, simple scripts from memory. Doesn't see the need for complex outlining or AI scripting.
personality: Charming, simple, dislikes technology, prefers analog.
triggers: A tool that is literally one click and doesn't require reading a manual.
objections: I don't understand why I need an app for this. My voice recorder is enough.
